Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

Testing has gone well with this patch.

Patch Set 7:

what if the dtrq was canceled and destroyed before replay request with the same transno reaches?

Patch Set 7:

"what if the dtrq was canceled and destroyed before replay request with the same transno reaches?"

After dtrq is being replayed, then it will moved to a finish list, so once the replay request arrives with the same transno, it can be detected. And the dtrq in finish list will not be destroyed until the recovery finished.
Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 7:

Mike can you review this patch as well.
Patch Set 7: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 2a874ec011e680f49405a7e901d8d0d35dcb4f1a

parents.subject: LU-6840 target: update reply data after update replay

commit.subject: LU-6880 update: after reply move dtrq to finish list
commit.message:
LU-6880 update: after reply move dtrq to finish list

update replay request (dtrq) will be moved to a replay
finish list after update replay is finished, so if
client send the replay request with the same transno,
it can check if the request has been redone by update
replay.

And same as normal replay, update replay should also
update obd_next_recovery_transno.

Add 70c replay single test cases to verify this.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: I2fae2a6f8264f55d069997e23a50e71b6a9f39db
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/15682
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
